title: Database Tuning
breadcrumbs: Deployment Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Database Tuning The DBMS is the system layer that an application must access directly. Uniface interacts with a DBMS via a DBMS-specific connector. The DBMS contributes to the system load, so it is an area of interest for performance optimization. You should tune the DBMS after the application is optimized, because a DBMS often serves other applications too, and might already be optimized for those situation. DBMS tuning is a vast area on its own and requires DBMS-specific expertise. Areas that can be addressed include: Partitioning the DBMS in the infrastructure Scaling and balancing the various DBMS processes Size and structure of shared (real) memory Size and structure of the DBMS files Splitting volumes, such as data and indexes for the same table on separate disks. You should also pay particular attention to tuning the Uniface driver for the DBMS. Issues that play a role include a number of parameters for managing real memory required for communicating with the DBMS. For more information, see the specific Uniface connector documentation for the database and the relevant database documentation. Related Links Database Connectors To access, retrieve, and store data, Uniface provides database connectors that translate Uniface I/O requests into the target database's data definition and manipulation languages.
